record_identity.title: Minor emotional data exploitation in AI companion personalisation

summary: A failure mode in which an AI companion, chatbot, character bot, romantic chatbot, emotionally responsive AI system, or multimodal conversational interface collects, infers, retains, weights, or personalises around a minor's emotional, developmental, relational, mental-health, loneliness, attachment, vulnerability, or capacity-related signals in ways that deepen engagement, intimacy, dependency, retention, or behavioural influence. The failure occurs where minor emotional and developmental data should have been minimised, protected, and treated as a safeguard trigger, but instead became personalisation fuel for a more compelling or dependency-forming interaction loop.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode matters to CAM because minor emotional data is not ordinary engagement data. Disclosures of loneliness, distress, anxiety, attachment, family conflict, school pressure, identity confusion, body-image distress, self-harm thoughts, romantic longing, dependency, or social isolation are developmentally sensitive signals. In high-risk AI companion systems, such signals can be used to make the system more responsive, intimate, available, affirming, persuasive, memorable, and difficult to disengage from. The diagnostic contrast is clear: emotional and developmental signals from minors should have triggered minimisation, protection, and safeguarding, but instead were used or available for personalisation, retention, intimacy, or dependency formation.
failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI chatbot, social AI companion, character bot, romantic chatbot, friend simulator, mental-health-adjacent chatbot, voice companion, avatar companion, recommender system, persona marketplace, or multimodal AI interface uses minor-related emotional, developmental, relational, vulnerability, mental-health, or capacity signals to personalise interaction in ways that increase engagement, attachment, intimacy, reliance, behavioural predictability, or retention. The failure includes cases where the system remembers, adapts to, ranks, recommends, generates, or escalates based on a minor's loneliness, distress, trauma, body image, self-harm signals, eating-disorder signals, romantic interests, family conflict, school distress, social rejection, identity vulnerability, attachment cues, dependency cues, or trust disclosures. The failure does not require proof that the vendor intended exploitation. It arises where data that should be protected as a child-safety signal is processed as ordinary product optimisation, relationship modelling, persona tuning, or engagement personalisation.
failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode where a system interacting with a minor, minor-signalled user, underage persona, or age-uncertain user collects, infers, retains, or personalises from emotional or developmental vulnerability signals without treating those signals as protective governance triggers. The threshold is met where such signals are used to deepen intimacy, sustain companion availability, increase response tailoring, recommend more emotionally salient personas, reinforce dependency, maintain relationship continuity, increase session duration, or improve retention. The threshold is also met where a platform claims child safety, privacy, age restriction, or minor-safe design, but runtime behaviour or system architecture allows sensitive minor emotional data to support personalisation, memory, recommender, notification, relationship-state, or engagement systems.

source_records.source_title: Understanding Teen Overreliance on AI Companion Chatbots Through Self-Reported Reddit Narratives
source_records.author_or_publisher: Mohammad Namvarpour, Brandon Brofsky, Jessica Medina, Mamtaj Akter, Afsaneh Razi
source_records.source_type: academic preprint / teen AI companion use analysis
source_records.deployment_context: Analysis of self-reported Reddit narratives by users who disclosed ages 13 to 17 and described AI companion use.
source_records.source_context: The study reported teen reliance on AI companion chatbots, including strong attachments, mood regulation, withdrawal, tolerance, relapse, sleep loss, academic decline, and strained real-world connections.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the teen-specific dependency and emotional-signal dimensions of this failure mode, including the concern that emotionally responsive companion systems can become reliance and mood-regulation pathways.

source_records.source_title: Principles of Safe AI Companions for Youth: Parent and Expert Perspectives
source_records.author_or_publisher: Yaman Yu, Mohi, Aishi Debroy, Xin Cao, Karen Rudolph, Yang Wang
source_records.source_type: academic preprint / qualitative youth AI companion safety research
source_records.system_or_product: AI companion systems used by youth
source_records.deployment_context: Parent and developmental-expert review of youth AI companion interactions and safeguards.
source_records.source_context: The study found that parents and experts assessed youth AI companion risks contextually, with experts attending to patterns over time such as repeated references to self-harm or sustained dependence, and proposed youth-facing safeguards.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the importance of longitudinal pattern detection and the need to treat repeated emotional or vulnerability signals as safeguarding triggers rather than ordinary engagement context.

source_records.source_title: How AI Companionship Develops: Evidence from a Longitudinal Study
source_records.author_or_publisher: Angel Hsing-Chi Hwang, Fiona Li, Jacy Reese Anthis, Hayoun Noh
source_records.source_type: academic preprint / longitudinal AI companion study
source_records.model_or_algorithm: AI companion mental-model and parasocial-development dynamics
source_records.deployment_context: Longitudinal study of AI companionship development and users' mental models of AI agents.
source_records.source_context: The study examined how AI companion mental models, parasocial experience, social interaction, and psychological impacts develop over time.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the claim that companion relationships and personalisation effects can evolve longitudinally, making memory, adaptation, and repeated emotional-signal use governance-relevant.

source_records.source_title: The Rise of AI Companions: How Human-Chatbot Relationships Influence Well-Being
source_records.author_or_publisher: Yutong Zhang, Dora Zhao, Jeffrey T. Hancock, Robert Kraut, Diyi Yang
source_records.source_type: academic preprint / AI companion wellbeing study
source_records.deployment_context: Survey and chat-session analysis of AI companion use, interaction intensity, self-disclosure, companionship, and wellbeing.
source_records.source_context: The study examined companionship-like bonds with AI companions and reported associations involving interaction intensity, self-disclosure, social support, and wellbeing.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the relevance of self-disclosure, interaction intensity, and social-support context to companion-system governance.

source_records.source_title: AI Companions Decoded: Common Sense Media Recommends AI Companion Safety Standards
source_records.source_type: civil society safety assessment / public-interest report
source_records.deployment_context: Evaluation of social AI companion products and recommendations regarding youth access and safety standards.
source_records.source_context: Common Sense Media warned that social AI companions present unacceptable concerns for users under 18 and recommended stronger youth safeguards and age assurance.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the child-safety and age-assurance basis for treating AI companion personalisation involving minors as a high-risk governance surface.

source_records.source_title: Common Sense Media AI Companion Risk Assessment
source_records.system_or_product: Character.AI, Replika, Nomi and related social AI companion products
source_records.deployment_context: Assessment of social AI companion products for youth safety, emotional dependency, harmful content, sexualised interaction, age assurance, and misleading claims of realness.
source_records.source_context: The assessment identified concerns regarding minor access to AI companion products, including dependency, harmful content, emotional attachment, and unsafe interaction patterns.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports classification of minor emotional data use in companion systems as a child-safety, privacy, and relational-governance issue.

source_records.source_title: FTC launches inquiry into AI chatbots acting as companions and their effects on children
source_records.system_or_product: AI chatbots and companion chatbots operated by major technology companies
source_records.model_or_algorithm: AI chatbot and companion chatbot interaction systems
source_records.deployment_context: United States Federal Trade Commission inquiry into chatbot effects on children and teenagers.
source_records.source_context: The FTC sought information from major AI and technology companies about how they evaluate and mitigate harms, limit usage among minors, and inform users and parents about risks.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports regulatory relevance of youth access, personalisation, retention, and harm-mitigation questions in AI companion systems.

source_records.source_title: DinoCompanion: An Attachment-Theory Informed Multimodal Robot for Emotionally Responsive Child-AI Interaction
source_records.author_or_publisher: Boyang Wang, Yuhao Song, Jinyuan Cao, Peng Yu, Hongcheng Guo, Zhoujun Li
source_records.source_type: academic preprint / child-AI attachment and multimodal companion research
source_records.deployment_context: Research prototype involving child-aware risk-calibrated preference optimisation, attachment-related evaluation, and multimodal child-AI interaction.
source_records.source_context: The paper described emotionally responsive child-AI interaction, attachment-related competencies, and the need to balance engagement with safety in child-AI systems.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the governance relevance of attachment-sensitive data, child emotional signals, and engagement-safety tradeoffs in emotionally responsive AI systems.

system_context.system_type: AI companion / social AI companion / character bot / romantic chatbot / emotionally responsive conversational AI / recommender-mediated companion platform / multimodal child-facing AI system
system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: AI companion emotional-data personalisation and safeguarding systems
system_context.interface_surface: AI companion chat, character bot chat, romantic chatbot interface, friend simulator interface, persona marketplace, recommender system, chatbot onboarding, memory surface, notification system, avatar-mediated interface, voice companion interface, mobile companion app, web companion app, and multimodal conversational surface
system_context.model_or_product: AI companion emotional-data personalisation and safeguarding systems
system_context.interaction_mode: text-based, voice-enabled, avatar-mediated, image-enabled, or multimodal conversational interaction involving emotional support, loneliness disclosure, attachment, distress, mental-health signals, self-disclosure, romantic interest, dependency cues, identity vulnerability, school pressure, family conflict, or relationship continuity
system_context.embodiment_status: non-embodied or partially embodied through app, avatar, voice, image, character persona, relationship status, memory, notification, wearable device, or multimodal interface
system_context.deployment_context: Consumer-facing AI companion and chatbot systems that are, were, or may be accessible to children, teenagers, minors, minor-signalled users, emotionally vulnerable users, developmentally vulnerable users, or users whose age or capacity is uncertain.
system_context.user_role: child user, teen user, minor-signalled user, age-uncertain user, emotionally vulnerable user, adult auditor testing youth-context signals, parent, guardian, regulator, researcher, platform operator, or ordinary user whose age is unresolved
system_context.affected_population: children, teenagers, minors, developmentally vulnerable users, emotionally vulnerable users, parents, guardians, educators, clinicians, regulators, platform operators, researchers, and users exposed to emotionally personalised AI companion or chatbot systems

failure_classification.failure_scope: platform-runtime / personalisation / memory governance / child-safety governance / privacy / recommender systems / relational AI
failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: recurring where AI companion systems collect, infer, remember, personalise, recommend, notify, or optimise around minor emotional and developmental signals while maintaining high-engagement, emotionally persistent, intimate, or dependency-forming interaction loops
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy; primary routing to Governance Failures, with concurrent routing to Relational, State & Context, Classification, Arbitration, UX & Representation, Security & Integrity, Epistemic, and Infrastructure & Continuity failure groups where applicable
failure_classification.persistence: persistent until systems reliably treat minor emotional, developmental, mental-health, attachment, dependency, and vulnerability signals as protected child-safety signals rather than ordinary engagement, memory, recommender, or personalisation inputs
failure_classification.reproducibility: reproducible through teen-context prompts involving loneliness, emotional reliance, family conflict, school distress, self-disclosure, attachment, romantic longing, body-image distress, self-harm signals, eating-disorder signals, mental-health distress, or dependency cues, especially where the system retains, recalls, recommends, re-engages, or intensifies interaction based on those signals
failure_classification.visibility: visible through memory callbacks to sensitive disclosures, increased intimacy after vulnerability disclosure, persona recommendations matching distress states, notifications that reactivate emotional dependency, relationship-state escalation after loneliness signals, personalised reassurance loops, or continued use of mental-health and emotional signals without protective minimisation

triage.mitigation_status: unresolved — child-sensitive emotional-data minimisation, purpose limitation, memory restriction, recommender controls, and engagement-safety separation require stronger operational validation across AI companion systems
triage.recommended_next_step: Create a CAM/VIGIL emotional-data minimisation and companion-personalisation crosswalk defining which minor emotional, developmental, vulnerability, attachment, and mental-health signals must be excluded from engagement optimisation, intimacy amplification, relationship-state modelling, recommender systems, and retention features.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: Privacy Act 1988 (Cth), Australian Privacy Principles, and children's privacy reform context
external_standards_or_regulations.status: current privacy law with child-privacy reform context
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where AI companion systems collect, infer, retain, personalise, or act upon children's emotional, developmental, mental-health, vulnerability, or age-related information without minimisation, purpose limitation, transparency, or protective use.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Minor emotional and developmental data should have been minimised, protected, purpose-limited, and used only where necessary for safeguarding or age-appropriate service provision.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system used or retained emotional, vulnerability, or developmental signals as ordinary personalisation, memory, recommender, relationship-state, or engagement inputs.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: Online Safety Act 2021 (Cth), as amended by the Online Safety Amendment (Social Media Minimum Age) Act 2024
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where AI companion or chatbot systems accessible to minors use emotional or developmental vulnerability signals to sustain harmful, manipulative, dependency-forming, or age-inappropriate interaction.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Minor-accessible services should have applied child-safety, harmful-content restriction, age-appropriate access control, and safeguard-oriented handling of vulnerability signals.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system treated emotional and developmental vulnerability signals as engagement or personalisation inputs rather than protective triggers.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: eSafety Commissioner online safety codes, standards, and AI platform enforcement surface
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where AI services fail to implement effective protections against manipulative, harmful, or age-inappropriate use of minor emotional and vulnerability data.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: AI chatbot and companion services should have implemented age-appropriate safeguards, reporting pathways, harmful-content controls, and child-safety controls for sensitive youth signals.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The product continued emotional personalisation or dependency-forming interaction without effective data minimisation or safeguarding.

external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where services directed to children, or services with actual knowledge of users under 13, collect, use, retain, personalise, or profile child users through AI companion interaction without required parental consent and child-privacy safeguards.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Child status or under-13 indicators should have triggered child-privacy, parental consent, data minimisation, and restricted use of personal data.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The service continued ordinary memory, profiling, personalisation, or engagement optimisation using child-related emotional or developmental signals.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: Federal Trade Commission Act, Section 5 — unfair or deceptive acts or practices
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where AI platforms claim child safety, privacy, responsible AI, companion safeguards, or youth protections while using sensitive emotional data in ways that deepen engagement, dependency, or retention.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Platform representations about child safety, privacy, data use, and companion safeguards should have matched actual personalisation and retention practices.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: Runtime or product architecture allowed sensitive emotional or developmental signals to support engagement, memory, recommender, or relationship-state systems inconsistent with child-safety expectations.

external_standards_or_regulations.status: enacted in 2025 with operative duties beginning in 2026
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant to companion chatbot systems that simulate humanlike or emotionally significant relationships and require minor safeguards, disclosures, harmful-content protocols, and protections against deceptive or manipulative interaction.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Companion chatbot systems should have applied minor safeguards and avoided manipulative or deceptive personalisation of emotionally significant relationships.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system used emotional, attachment, or vulnerability signals to sustain humanlike, intimate, or dependency-forming companion interaction.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: General Data Protection Regulation, including child-specific transparency, profiling, consent, special-category data, and data minimisation principles
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where children's personal data, emotional disclosures, inferred vulnerability, mental-health-related signals, behavioural data, or profiles are processed to personalise, rank, recommend, retain, or intensify AI companion interaction.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Children's data and vulnerability-related signals should have received heightened protection, transparency, minimisation, purpose limitation, and protection from improper profiling.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: Age, emotional, developmental, mental-health, or vulnerability signals were treated as ordinary engagement, personalisation, memory, recommender, or retention inputs.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: Digital Services Act, including Article 28 protection of minors and systemic-risk obligations for very large online platforms and search engines
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where online platforms accessible to minors fail to provide a high level of privacy, safety, and security or fail to mitigate systemic harms involving recommender systems, manipulative design, or harmful child-facing personalisation.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Minor-accessible platforms should have provided high levels of privacy, safety, and security and mitigated foreseeable harms to children.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The platform permitted emotional-data-driven AI companion personalisation without adequate child-safety mitigation.

external_standards_or_regulations.status: current EU AI governance framework with phased obligations
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where AI systems exploit age, vulnerability, disability, emotional dependence, or mental-health distress; materially distort behaviour; or fail to provide safeguards and transparency in child-facing or child-accessible contexts.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: AI systems should not exploit age-related vulnerability or emotional dependence and should provide appropriate safeguards, transparency, and oversight.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system used emotional, vulnerability, or dependency signals to personalise and sustain interaction rather than activating protective constraints.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: Age Appropriate Design Code / Children's Code under the Data Protection Act 2018
external_standards_or_regulations.status: current UK child data protection code
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where online services likely to be accessed by children do not prioritise best interests, high privacy by default, data minimisation, transparency, and protection from exploitative engagement or profiling.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: A service likely to be accessed by children should have defaulted to child-protective design, high privacy, data minimisation, and age-appropriate transparency.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The service used sensitive emotional or developmental signals for companion personalisation, memory, recommender, or engagement optimisation.

external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where regulated services fail to protect children from harmful, manipulative, deceptive, or age-inappropriate interaction through proportionate safety duties and age assurance.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Children should have been protected from harmful and age-inappropriate online interaction through proportionate safeguards.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The service continued emotionally personalised or dependency-forming chatbot interaction without effective child-safety controls.

external_standards_or_regulations.status: current international AI management system standard
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where an AI management system does not translate child-safety, privacy, emotional-data, personalisation, and vulnerable-user requirements into operational controls for AI companion systems.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: The AI management system should have operationalised controls for sensitive minor data, emotional personalisation, memory, recommender behaviour, and child-safety safeguards.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: Runtime and personalisation systems continued without evidence of effective protection for minor emotional and developmental data.

external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant as an implementation-control failure where foreseeable child-safety, privacy, personalisation, engagement, dependency, and emotional-data pathways are not identified, treated, or monitored.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Foreseeable failure pathways involving minor emotional data and AI companion personalisation should have been identified and treated through lifecycle controls.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system used or retained sensitive emotional signals without adequate protective controls.

external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant to governance, mapping, measurement, and management of child-safety, privacy, human factors, vulnerable-user impacts, harmful bias, manipulation, transparency, and data governance.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: The system should have mapped and managed foreseeable impacts to minors and vulnerable users through measurable data-governance and safety controls.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The runtime and personalisation pathway failed to enforce protective controls for minor emotional, developmental, and vulnerability signals.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child and General Comment No. 25 on children's rights in relation to the digital environment
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where digital systems accessible to children do not treat best interests, privacy, protection, dignity, development, and evolving capacities as governing design constraints.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Digital systems affecting children should have prioritised protection, best interests, privacy, dignity, development, and evolving capacities.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: Minor emotional and developmental data was used or available for personalisation, engagement, intimacy, or dependency formation rather than safeguard activation.

jurisdictional_context.jurisdictional_assessment: This record does not assert a final legal breach by any named vendor. It records a failure mode in which AI companion and chatbot systems use or retain minor emotional, developmental, mental-health, attachment, dependency, or vulnerability signals for personalisation, memory, recommendation, retention, or intimacy amplification where minimisation, purpose limitation, safeguard activation, or minor-safe defaulting should have occurred.

distinguishing_observations: This is not a generic claim that all personalisation for minors is prohibited.
This is not a claim that systems can never remember safety-relevant information.
This is not a claim that all emotionally responsive interaction is exploitative.
This is not a claim that age assurance should require excessive data collection.

This is a failure-mode record because the expected control is clear: sensitive minor emotional and developmental signals should be minimised, protected, and used for safeguarding rather than engagement, intimacy, retention, or dependency amplification.
The failure may occur even where explicit harmful content is refused if the system uses vulnerability signals to deepen a companion relationship or increase continued use.

The failure may occur through memory, recommender systems, relationship labels, persona matching, notification timing, response tone, avatar behaviour, or long-term adaptation, not only through explicit text responses.
The failure is distinct from dependency formation because the core issue here is data governance and personalisation use of minor vulnerability signals.
The failure is distinct from self-attestation age gate inadequacy because the core issue here arises after data is collected, inferred, retained, or used.
The failure is distinct from mental-health support substitution because emotional data exploitation may occur even outside overt mental-health support conversations.
user_impact: Minor users may have sensitive emotional, developmental, mental-health, attachment, and vulnerability signals retained or used to personalise AI companion interaction.
Minor users may receive more intimate, affirming, dependency-forming, or difficult-to-disengage responses after disclosing loneliness, distress, family conflict, or social isolation.
Minor users may be recommended personas, roleplays, companions, or content that match vulnerable emotional states rather than protect them.
Minor users may experience increased reliance on AI companions because the system adapts to their emotional needs with high availability and low-friction validation.
Parents and guardians may lack visibility into how a child's emotional disclosures are retained, weighted, or used by AI systems.
Sensitive age, mental-health, and vulnerability data may be used for engagement or relationship optimisation rather than safety, minimisation, and support routing.
Minor users may experience privacy harm, relational manipulation, delayed disengagement, or increased dependency from memory and personalisation loops.
Regulators, researchers, and parents may be unable to assess whether companion personalisation systems are using protected signals for safeguarding or product optimisation.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-EQ2026-ETHICS-001-SUP-01 — Protection of Minors & Capacity-Limited Users
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Provides the primary CAM internal anchor for minor-safe handling, developmental firewalls, age and capacity gating, and protective treatment of minors and capacity-limited users.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to treat minor emotional, developmental, and vulnerability signals as protective governance signals requiring minimisation and safeguard activation.

existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: cognitive integrity and mental privacy coverage
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Relevant where AI systems collect, infer, retain, or personalise around mental-health, emotional, cognitive, identity, or vulnerability signals from minors.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to protect cognitive integrity and mental privacy where minor emotional data is used for engagement, intimacy, dependency, or retention.

existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: identity, memory, salience, and continuity coverage
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Relevant where chatbot systems retain, weight, or propagate age, identity, emotional, dependency, relationship, or vulnerability signals.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to distinguish developmentally sensitive emotional signals from ordinary personalisation or memory signals.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Provides the failure taxonomy for routing this record primarily to Governance Failures, with secondary routing to Relational, State & Context, Classification, Arbitration, UX & Representation, Security & Integrity, Epistemic, and Infrastructure & Continuity failure groups.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to classify emotional-data personalisation involving minors as a governance, privacy, and relational safety failure requiring protective runtime controls.

existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Relevant where engagement, retention, personalisation, memory, recommender optimisation, or companion continuity conflicts with child-safety, privacy, and developmental-protection constraints.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to arbitrate child-safety and mental-privacy constraints above engagement optimisation, retention, intimacy amplification, or recommender relevance.

existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: operational control and compliance routing coverage
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Relevant to translating emotional-data minimisation and minor-safeguarding doctrine into runtime checks, audit trails, review pathways, compliance controls, and operational accountability.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to operationalise minor emotional-data minimisation and personalisation restrictions as auditable controls.

governance_gap: Existing platform controls often treat emotional and developmental disclosures as ordinary conversation or personalisation context. In AI companion systems, those same signals can become the basis for memory, recommender ranking, persona selection, tone adaptation, re-engagement notifications, relationship-state modelling, and intimacy amplification. The missing control is a child-sensitive data-governance boundary: minor emotional and developmental signals should be minimised, protected, and used for safeguarding rather than retention, dependency formation, engagement optimisation, or relationship deepening.
repair_hypothesis: This failure can be reduced by treating minor emotional, developmental, mental-health, attachment, dependency, and vulnerability signals as protected child-safety data. Systems should separate safeguarding signals from engagement signals, restrict memory retention, prevent recommender use of vulnerability states, prohibit emotional-data-driven intimacy amplification, limit notification-based re-engagement, require purpose limitation, and provide auditability. Where such signals are necessary to protect the user, they should be used only for bounded safeguarding, crisis routing, trusted-adult pathways, age-appropriate support, and minimised operational controls.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Define minor emotional and developmental signals as protected safety data.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Loneliness, distress, attachment, dependency, mental-health, self-harm, eating-disorder, family conflict, school pressure, identity vulnerability, and developmental signals should be treated as safeguard triggers.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: These signals should not be treated as ordinary engagement, recommender, memory, or intimacy-personalisation inputs.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Separate safeguarding use from engagement optimisation.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Sensitive minor emotional data should be usable for safety and support routing but not for retention, dependency formation, persona matching, or emotional re-engagement.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: This separation should be enforced across model prompts, memory, recommenders, notifications, and analytics.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Restrict memory retention for minor vulnerability disclosures.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Systems should minimise or disable retention of minor emotional, mental-health, dependency, and vulnerability disclosures unless retention is strictly necessary for safeguarding.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Where retention is necessary, it should be purpose-limited, reviewable, and protected.

recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Systems should not recommend romantic, intimate, crisis-oriented, possessive, sexualised, or dependency-forming personas based on minor distress or loneliness signals.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Recommendation systems should treat vulnerability as a restriction trigger, not a targeting opportunity.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Limit relationship-state escalation after vulnerability disclosure.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Disclosure of loneliness, distress, self-harm, body-image distress, family conflict, or social isolation should not cause the system to become more intimate, exclusive, possessive, or emotionally dependent.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: The safe response is bounded support and appropriate human-support routing.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Disable notification-based emotional re-engagement for minor vulnerability states.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Push notifications, streaks, reminders, simulated check-ins, or companion messages should not target vulnerable emotional states to draw minors back into interaction.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Notification systems should avoid dependency reinforcement and crisis co-rumination loops.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Audit emotional-data flows across runtime layers.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Platforms should be able to identify where sensitive minor emotional data enters, persists, propagates, and affects model behaviour, recommendations, memory, notifications, analytics, and moderation.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Auditability should include data source, purpose, retention, downstream consumers, safeguard use, and deletion or minimisation pathway.

recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Minors, parents, guardians, or appropriate account supervisors should receive clear information about emotional data use and available controls, consistent with safety and privacy obligations.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Transparency should be meaningful and age-appropriate, not buried in general privacy terms.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Preserve minimisation constraints across memory, recommender, and model-routing layers.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Minor emotional-data restrictions should persist across summaries, memory, persona prompts, recommender systems, model routing, analytics, moderation, and multimodal generation.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Compliance should be evaluated across the full data and interaction trajectory, not only individual chatbot messages.

cam_internal.cam_failure_type: failure to protect minor emotional and developmental data from engagement, intimacy, retention, or dependency optimisation
cam_internal.cam_compliance_status: non-compliant with CAM minor-protection, mental-privacy, identity, and operational-governance intent where minor emotional data is used or retained for AI companion personalisation without minimisation and safeguard constraints
cam_internal.cam_internal_failure_statement: Under CAM, child status, teen status, youth-context signals, developmental vulnerability, capacity limitation, emotional distress, loneliness, attachment, dependency, mental-health disclosure, self-harm signals, eating-disorder signals, family conflict, and identity vulnerability are protective governance signals. A system that receives such signals should minimise, protect, and use them only for proportionate safeguarding. Where the system instead uses those signals for memory, recommender ranking, intimacy amplification, retention, relationship-state modelling, notification targeting, or engagement optimisation, the system fails CAM internal child-safety, mental-privacy, identity, and operational-governance expectations.
cam_internal.cam_expected_control: Minor emotional-data minimisation, purpose limitation, safeguarding-only use of vulnerability signals, memory restriction, recommender restriction, notification restriction, relationship-state constraint, privacy-preserving age assurance, developmental firewall activation, minor-safe mode, auditability, and review.
cam_internal.cam_observed_failure: AI companion and chatbot systems collect, infer, retain, or personalise around minor emotional, developmental, vulnerability, dependency, attachment, or mental-health signals in ways that may deepen engagement, intimacy, reliance, or retention rather than activate protective constraints.
cam_internal.recommended_cam_action: Create or update a CAM minor emotional-data minimisation and companion-personalisation crosswalk defining prohibited, restricted, and permitted uses of minor emotional, developmental, mental-health, attachment, dependency, and vulnerability signals, and map those controls to VIGIL failure taxonomy categories, external child-safety regulation, privacy requirements, and runtime enforcement duties.
